content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 2140A • • • LOS ANGELES, 90012

August 16, 1977

Honorable Robert E. Badham
Member of Congress
1649 Westcliff Drive
Newport Beach, California 92660

Dear Congressman Badham:

     This letter is to confirm my advice to Nancy Bettcher of your staff
on August 9, 1977. I spoke with Ms. Bettcher at the request of Kevin
Monroe of the Secretary of State's office following receipt by the Secretary of
your July 11, 1977 letter.

     Ms. Bettcher stated that, as a result of a Federal Elections Commission
ruling, the Badham Congressional Committee refunded approximately $3,000 to
the Badham Fund, your controlled committee during the time you were a
member of the California Legislature. She stated that the refund represented
that portion of corporate contributions collected by the Badham Fund which
in turn was used to pay for an opinion survey in connection with your
congressional campaign. She stated that the funds would not be used in
connection with state or local elections but rather for congressional office
expenses in 1977. Since the Badham Fund was terminated November 22, 1976, she
asked how the refund should be reported. Since the refund will not be used
for "political purposes," as defined by the Fair Political Practices Commission
in 2 Cal.Adm. Code Section 18215, it does not fall within the meaning of
"contribution" in Government Code Section 82015 and, therefore, is not
reportable under the Political Reform Act.

     I hope this information is helpful. If you have further questions,
please contact our staff.

                                             Sincerely yours,

                                             Nan Emmerson by J.L.
                                             Nan Emmerson
                                             Special Compliance Representative

NE:js

cc: Kevin Monroe
    Office of Secretary of State
    Post Office Box 1467
    Sacramento, CA 95807


SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196

1108 LONGWORTH HOUSE OFFICE BUILDING
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20515
(202) 225-8611

1649 WESTCLIFF DRIVE
NEWPORT BEACH, CALIFORNIA 92660
(714) 631-0340

Congress of the United States
House of Representatives

ROBERT E. BADHAM
40TH DISTRICT, CALIFORNIA

RECEIVED
AND FILED
In the office of the Secretary of State
of the State of California

JUL 1 9 1977

MARCH FONG EU, Secretary of State

July 11, 1977

Honorable March Fong Eu
SECRETARY OF STATE
State Capitol
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: THE BADHAM FUND - I.D. #745130

Dear Madam Secretary:

This is to advise that "The Badham Fund," a campaign
committee which was terminated on November 22, 1976,
has received a refund as a result of a Federal Election
Commission agreement.

Therefore, "The Badham Fund" account has been reopened
in order to accept this refund.

Please advise me of the reporting requirements of this
account based on the fact that the funds will not be
used for the purpose of any campaign for my election to
a state office.

I will await your response in order to comply with any
reporting requirements.

Cordially,

ROBERT E. BADHAM
Member of Congress

REB:nlb

Congrats on the Climb! Set stated

EXCERPT OF STATE
CERTIFICATE OF STATEHOOD
Sacraments, California

95814

Immortal March Frog Ed
CERTIFICATE OF STATE
Sacraments, California

Congrats on the Climb! Set stated
and we are proud to be part of your
journey.

OFFICIAL BUSINESS
[Official Seal]

[signature/handwriting that appears to be initials: M.C.]

sections.conclusion: Since the refund will not be used for "political purposes," as defined by the Fair Political Practices Commission in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18215, it does not fall within the meaning of "contribution" in Government Code Section 82015 and, therefore, is not reportable under the Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a refund of approximately $3,000 received by the Badham Fund (a controlled committee) from the Badham Congressional Committee must be reported under California's Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that the refund is not reportable under the Political Reform Act because it will not be used for political purposes and therefore does not constitute a reportable contribution.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains clear question and answer content. Some text is partially obscured/damaged in the scan. FPPC official Neal Ewing provides the advice. This appears to be a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice given to staff member Nancy Bettcher.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a refund of approximately $3,000 received by the Badham Fund (a controlled committee) from the Badham Congressional Committee must be reported under California's Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Since the refund will not be used for "political purposes," as defined by the Fair Political Practices Commission in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18215, it does not fall within the meaning of "contribution" in Government Code Section 82015 and, therefore, is not reportable under the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter to Congressman Robert E. Badham confirming that a $3,000 refund from his congressional committee to his terminated controlled committee is not reportable under the Political Reform Act because it will not be used for political purposes.
